assessments.rationale: Product is certified to EN 179 / EN 1125 (single-motion). NFPA 101 Life Safety Code and IBC Chapter 10 impose equivalent requirements for US campuses. The product's design pre-satisfies both regimes.
NFPA 101 requires emergency egress devices to function under power failure. The Türwächter's mechanical alarm trigger operates at 0V by design — satisfies the absolute constraint in full.

University buildings — particularly 1950s-1970s reinforced concrete construction — regularly exceed the 30 m integrated TX range. Multi-wing dormitories, research lab clusters, and lecture halls require repeater stages. Known engineering solution with available hardware.
assessments.mitigation: Conduct per-building radio survey during installation planning to identify coverage gaps; Deploy GfS repeater units (+30 m per stage) in stairwells and corridor junctions; For large buildings (>60 m footprint), install GfS wireless BUS gateway at building core

assessments.rationale: Dormitory stairwell exits and high-traffic academic corridor exits see frequent door events, increasing current draw on the 9V battery. Campus CMMS infrastructure enables systematic PM scheduling.
assessments.mitigation: Specify battery-monitoring variant to surface battery-low alerts before failure; Integrate battery-low alerts into campus CMMS for preventive maintenance scheduling; Classify high-traffic exits as quarterly inspection points

assessments.rationale: Surface-mount installation is an advantage in this market. One-third of US campus buildings exceed 50 years of age; retrofitting wired or flush-mount hardware requires renovation permits, asbestos/lead surveys, and construction disruption.

University SOC platforms (Lenel, Genetec) expect IP-native security device integration. EasyWave requires a GfS BUS gateway bridge and custom API connector to enter the PSIM event stream. Solvable but requires explicit project scope and budget.
assessments.mitigation: Deploy GfS wireless BUS gateway to aggregate EasyWave events; Develop REST/Webhook integration from GfS gateway to campus PSIM event feed; As interim fallback: wire gateway relay output to existing campus alarm panel input

assessments.rationale: At ~EUR 730/unit, a 100-door campus deployment reaches ~EUR 73K — above many public university's competitive bid threshold (~$25K). ROI case is achievable via Clery Act documentation savings and reduced security patrol.
assessments.mitigation: Develop campus-scale TCO model: IoT deployment vs. incremental security patrol FTE cost; Quantify Clery Act compliance labor savings; Structure campus deployment in phases (pilot 20-50 doors) to stay below competitive bid threshold

assessments.rationale: US campus emergency exit event logs are building-level data not linked to individual identities — not covered by FERPA. GDPR applies to EU campus deployments; standard DPA and DPIA suffice.
Primary campus installation environment is indoor corridors, stairwells, and hallways. IP45 outdoor variant serves exceptions (outdoor exits, pool areas). No new engineering required.

University campuses frequently combine access control with emergency exit compliance on the same door (lab wings, secure server rooms). System integrator certification of combined configurations is standard practice.
assessments.mitigation: Include NFPA 101 fail-safe egress review in campus door hardware specification for combined configurations; Specify EM holders as fail-open; Prepare AHJ documentation package for combined system configurations
